---
title: "Network Analysis + Dashboard Work Session"
subtitle: ": OSMnx, Routing, and Shiny Integration"
date: 04/22/2025
date-format: long
author:
  - name: Hyesop Shin
    orcid: 0000-0003-2637-7933
    email: hyesop.shin@auckland.ac.nz
    affiliations:
      - name: University of Auckland
        address: 23 Symonds Street
        city: Auckland
        postal-code: 1010
format: 
  revealjs:
      logo: Figs/uoa-logo.png
      css: logo.css
      slide-number: true
      show-slide-number: print
editor: source
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style type="text/css">
      ul li ul li {
        font-size: 0.7em;
      }
      </style>
---

## Note: ANZAC Week

* **Thursday 24 April**: ANZAC Day (Public Holiday)
* **No formal lecture** on Tuesday
* **Labs run as normal** - work session for Assignment 2
* **Today**: Network analysis tools for your dashboards

## Recap of Previous Lectures

* GeoPandas and spatial operations
* Shiny for Python basics
* Reactive programming
* Your first dashboards

## Today's Focus

::: {.incremental}
* **Part 1**: Network analysis with OSMnx (45 mins)
* **Part 2**: Routing with r5py (35 mins)
* **Part 3**: Integrating into Shiny dashboards (30 mins)
* **Remainder**: Assignment 2 work time
:::

## Assignment 2 Status Check

**Due**: 8 May (Week 8)

**Have you:**
- ✅ Chosen your topic?
- ✅ Created basic Shiny structure?
- ✅ Added at least one input and output?
- ✅ Started data collection/processing?

**Today**: Add network analysis component!

# Part 1: Network Analysis with OSMnx

## What is a Street Network?

![](https://geoffboeing.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/osmnx-street-network.png){fig-align=center}

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Graph Theory**:
- **Nodes** (intersections, dead ends)
- **Edges** (street segments)
- **Attributes** (length, speed limit, one-way)
:::

## Why OSMnx?

**OSMnx** = OpenStreetMap + NetworkX

::: {.incremental}
* Download street networks from anywhere
* Analyze network topology
* Calculate routes
* Visualize networks
* **Free and open source!**
:::

## Installing OSMnx

```python
# In Colab
!pip install osmnx

# Import
import osmnx as ox
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Configure
ox.settings.use_cache = True
ox.settings.log_console = True
```

## Downloading a Network

```python
# Download Auckland CBD street network
place = "Auckland CBD, New Zealand"

# Get walking network
G = ox.graph_from_place(
    place,
    network_type='walk'  # 'walk', 'bike', 'drive', 'all'
)

# Plot
fig, ax = ox.plot_graph(G, 
                         node_size=10,
                         node_color='red',
                         edge_color='grey',
                         edge_linewidth=0.5)
```

## Network Types

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
# Different network types for different analyses
G_walk = ox.graph_from_place(place, network_type='walk')
G_bike = ox.graph_from_place(place, network_type='bike')
G_drive = ox.graph_from_place(place, network_type='drive')
G_all = ox.graph_from_place(place, network_type='all')
```

**Choose based on your analysis:**
- **Pedestrian accessibility** → `'walk'`
- **Cycling infrastructure** → `'bike'`
- **Vehicle routing** → `'drive'`
- **General network** → `'all'`
:::

## Alternative: Bounding Box

```python
# Download by bounding box
north = -36.83
south = -36.87
east = 174.78
west = 174.75

G = ox.graph_from_bbox(
    north, south, east, west,
    network_type='walk'
)

# Or by distance from point
point = (-36.8485, 174.7633)  # Sky Tower
G = ox.graph_from_point(
    point,
    dist=1000,  # 1km radius
    network_type='walk'
)
```

## Network Statistics

```python
# Basic statistics
stats = ox.basic_stats(G)

print(f"Nodes: {stats['n']}")
print(f"Edges: {stats['m']}")
print(f"Total length: {stats['total_edge_length']/1000:.2f} km")
print(f"Average degree: {stats['k_avg']:.2f}")

# More detailed stats
stats_extended = ox.extended_stats(G)
print(f"Average street length: {stats_extended['street_length_avg']:.1f}m")
print(f"Intersection count: {stats_extended['intersection_count']}")
```

## Network Centrality

**Betweenness centrality**: Which streets are most important?

```python
# Calculate betweenness centrality
bc = nx.betweenness_centrality(G, weight='length')

# Add to edges
for u, v, k in G.edges(keys=True):
    G.edges[u, v, k]['betweenness'] = bc.get((u, v), 0)

# Get edge colors based on betweenness
ec = ox.plot.get_edge_colors_by_attr(
    G, 'betweenness', cmap='plasma'
)

# Plot
fig, ax = ox.plot_graph(G, edge_color=ec, 
                         node_size=0, edge_linewidth=1.5)
```

::: {.callout-note}
**High betweenness** = many shortest paths pass through it = important for connectivity
:::

## Closeness Centrality

**How accessible is each intersection?**

```python
# Closeness centrality
cc = nx.closeness_centrality(G, distance='length')

# Get node colors
nc = [cc[node] for node in G.nodes()]

# Plot
fig, ax = ox.plot_graph(G, 
                         node_color=nc,
                         node_size=30,
                         cmap='YlOrRd',
                         edge_color='grey',
                         edge_linewidth=0.5)
plt.title('Closeness Centrality (Accessibility)')
plt.show()
```

## Your Turn: Download and Analyze

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
1. Download street network for your favourite suburb
2. Calculate basic statistics
3. Calculate betweenness centrality
4. Create a visualization
5. **Bonus**: Which street is most central?

**Hint**: Use `ox.graph_from_place()` with place name
:::

# Part 2: Routing with r5py

## Why r5py?

**r5py** = Multi-modal transport routing

::: {.incremental}
* Purpose-built for transport analysis
* **Much faster** than OSMnx for routing
* Multi-modal (walk + bike + transit)
* Time-dependent routing
* Travel time matrices
:::

## Installing r5py

```python
# Requires Java JDK 11+
!apt-get install -y openjdk-11-jdk-headless

# Install r5py
!pip install r5py

# Import
import r5py
from r5py import TransportMode, LegMode
from datetime import datetime
```

## Setting Up r5py

```python
# Download OSM extract for Auckland
# From: https://download.geofabrik.de/australia-oceania.html

# Initialize transport network
transport_network = r5py.TransportNetwork(
    "auckland.osm.pbf",
    [  # Optional: GTFS feeds for public transport
        "at-gtfs.zip"
    ]
)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Download OSM extract from Geofabrik: geofabrik.de
:::

## Simple Route Calculation

```python
# Origin and destination
origin = (-36.8485, 174.7633)  # Sky Tower
destination = (-36.8447, 174.7680)  # University

# Calculate travel time
travel_time_calculator = r5py.TravelTimeMatrixComputer(
    transport_network,
    origins=[origin],
    destinations=[destination],
    departure_time=datetime(2025, 4, 22, 9, 0),  # 9am
    transport_modes=[LegMode.WALK]
)

# Get result
travel_times = travel_time_calculator.compute_travel_times()
print(f"Walking time: {travel_times['travel_time'].iloc[0]} minutes")
```

## Multi-Modal Routing

```python
# Walk + transit
travel_time_calculator = r5py.TravelTimeMatrixComputer(
    transport_network,
    origins=[origin],
    destinations=[destination],
    departure_time=datetime(2025, 4, 22, 9, 0),
    transport_modes=[
        LegMode.WALK,
        LegMode.TRANSIT
    ],
    max_time=datetime.timedelta(minutes=60)
)

travel_times = travel_time_calculator.compute_travel_times()
```

## Travel Time Matrix

**Calculate travel times to many destinations**

```python
# Many destinations (e.g., SA2 centroids)
origins_gdf = gpd.read_file('residential_points.gpkg')
destinations_gdf = gpd.read_file('sa2_centroids.gpkg')

# Calculate matrix
travel_time_calculator = r5py.TravelTimeMatrixComputer(
    transport_network,
    origins=origins_gdf[['geometry']],
    destinations=destinations_gdf[['geometry']],
    departure_time=datetime(2025, 4, 22, 9, 0),
    transport_modes=[LegMode.WALK, LegMode.TRANSIT]
)

# Result: DataFrame with origin, destination, travel_time
matrix = travel_time_calculator.compute_travel_times()
```

## Time-Dependent Routing

**Different times = different travel times!**

```python
# Peak hour (8am)
peak = r5py.TravelTimeMatrixComputer(
    transport_network,
    origins=[origin],
    destinations=destinations_gdf[['geometry']],
    departure_time=datetime(2025, 4, 22, 8, 0),  # 8am
    transport_modes=[LegMode.TRANSIT]
)

# Off-peak (11am)
offpeak = r5py.TravelTimeMatrixComputer(
    transport_network,
    origins=[origin],
    destinations=destinations_gdf[['geometry']],
    departure_time=datetime(2025, 4, 22, 11, 0),  # 11am
    transport_modes=[LegMode.TRANSIT]
)

# Compare
peak_times = peak.compute_travel_times()
offpeak_times = offpeak.compute_travel_times()
```

## Accessibility Analysis

```python
# How many jobs can you reach in 30 minutes?
jobs_gdf = gpd.read_file('employment_locations.gpkg')

# From each residential area
accessibility = []
for idx, home in origins_gdf.iterrows():
    # Calculate travel times
    calc = r5py.TravelTimeMatrixComputer(
        transport_network,
        origins=[home.geometry],
        destinations=jobs_gdf[['geometry']],
        departure_time=datetime(2025, 4, 22, 9, 0),
        transport_modes=[LegMode.WALK, LegMode.TRANSIT]
    )
    
    times = calc.compute_travel_times()
    
    # Count reachable in 30 mins
    reachable = times[times['travel_time'] <= 30]
    accessibility.append(len(reachable))

origins_gdf['jobs_30min'] = accessibility
```

## Your Turn: Routing

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
1. Calculate travel time from your home to university
2. Try different departure times (8am vs 11am)
3. Compare walk-only vs walk+transit
4. **Bonus**: Calculate to multiple destinations

**Note**: Requires OSM extract and Java setup
:::

# Part 3: Integrating with Shiny

## Network Visualization in Shiny

```python
from shiny import App, ui, render
import osmnx as ox

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.h2("Auckland Street Networks"),
    ui.input_select(
        "network_type",
        "Network Type:",
        choices=["walk", "bike", "drive"]
    ),
    ui.output_plot("network_map")
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @output
    @render.plot
    def network_map():
        # Download network based on selection
        G = ox.graph_from_place(
            "Auckland CBD, New Zealand",
            network_type=input.network_type()
        )
        
        fig, ax = ox.plot_graph(G, 
                                 node_size=5,
                                 edge_linewidth=0.5,
                                 figsize=(12, 12))
        return fig

app = App(app_ui, server)
```

## Interactive Centrality Analysis

```python
from shiny import reactive

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.h2("Network Centrality Analysis"),
    ui.input_select("measure", "Centrality Measure:",
                    choices=["betweenness", "closeness", "degree"]),
    ui.output_plot("centrality_map"),
    ui.output_text("stats")
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @reactive.calc
    def network():
        # Load network once
        return ox.graph_from_place("Auckland CBD, New Zealand",
                                   network_type='walk')
    
    @reactive.calc
    def centrality():
        G = network()
        measure = input.measure()
        
        if measure == "betweenness":
            return nx.betweenness_centrality(G, weight='length')
        elif measure == "closeness":
            return nx.closeness_centrality(G, distance='length')
        else:  # degree
            return dict(G.degree())
    
    @output
    @render.plot
    def centrality_map():
        G = network()
        c = centrality()
        
        # Color nodes by centrality
        node_colors = [c[node] for node in G.nodes()]
        
        fig, ax = ox.plot_graph(G, node_color=node_colors,
                                 node_size=30, cmap='plasma')
        return fig
```

## Routing Dashboard Example

```python
app_ui = ui.page_sidebar(
    ui.sidebar(
        ui.input_text("origin", "Origin:", 
                      value="Sky Tower, Auckland"),
        ui.input_text("destination", "Destination:", 
                      value="University of Auckland"),
        ui.input_select("mode", "Mode:",
                        choices=["walk", "bike", "drive"]),
        ui.input_action_button("calculate", "Calculate Route")
    ),
    ui.output_plot("route_map"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("route_info")
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @reactive.event(input.calculate)
    def route():
        # Geocode locations
        orig = ox.geocode(input.origin())
        dest = ox.geocode(input.destination())
        
        # Get network
        G = ox.graph_from_point(orig, dist=2000, 
                                network_type=input.mode())
        
        # Find nearest nodes
        orig_node = ox.distance.nearest_nodes(G, orig[1], orig[0])
        dest_node = ox.distance.nearest_nodes(G, dest[1], dest[0])
        
        # Calculate shortest path
        route_nodes = nx.shortest_path(G, orig_node, dest_node,
                                       weight='length')
        
        return G, route_nodes
    
    @output
    @render.plot
    def route_map():
        G, route_nodes = route()
        fig, ax = ox.plot_graph_route(G, route_nodes, 
                                       route_linewidth=4,
                                       node_size=0)
        return fig
```

## Assignment 2 Network Integration Ideas

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**Pedestrian Dashboard:**
- Show walking routes between sensors
- Calculate betweenness of sensor locations
- Identify important pedestrian corridors

**E-Scooter Analysis:**
- Network connectivity of trip origins/destinations
- Identify high-centrality locations for rebalancing
- Route clustering analysis

**Accessibility Dashboard:**
- Travel time isochrones
- Multi-modal routing options
- Job accessibility maps

**15-Minute City:**
- Calculate accessibility scores
- Identify underserved areas
- Compare different transport modes
:::

## Performance Tips

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**1. Cache network downloads**
```python
@reactive.calc
def network():
    # Only downloads once
    return ox.graph_from_place(place, network_type='walk')
```

**2. Limit analysis area**
```python
# Don't download all of Auckland!
G = ox.graph_from_point(center, dist=2000)  # 2km radius
```

**3. Save networks locally**
```python
# Save for reuse
ox.save_graphml(G, "auckland_cbd_walk.graphml")

# Load later
G = ox.load_graphml("auckland_cbd_walk.graphml")
```
:::

## Common Issues

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**1. Geocoding failures**
```python
# Be specific with place names
place = "Auckland CBD, Auckland, New Zealand"  # ✅
place = "Auckland"  # ❌ Too vague
```

**2. Empty networks**
```python
# Check before using
if len(G.nodes()) == 0:
    print("No network found! Check location and network_type")
```

**3. Memory issues**
```python
# Large networks can crash Colab
# Limit by distance or bbox
G = ox.graph_from_point(center, dist=1000)  # Not dist=10000!
```
:::

## Your Turn: Dashboard Component

::: {style="font-size: 100%;"}
**Build a simple network dashboard:**

1. Create Shiny app with network type selector
2. Download and display network
3. Add centrality calculation
4. Show statistics
5. **Bonus**: Add route calculation

**Time**: 30 minutes
**Ask for help if stuck!**
:::

# Assignment 2 Work Session

## Remaining Time

**Use lab time for:**
- Network analysis implementation
- Dashboard refinement
- Data processing
- Visualization improvements
- Testing and debugging

**Get help from:**
- Instructor
- TAs
- Classmates

## Assignment 2 Checklist

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**Week 7 Goals:**
- [ ] Multi-page structure complete
- [ ] Interactive map working
- [ ] At least 3 input widgets
- [ ] At least 3 output displays
- [ ] Network analysis (if applicable)
- [ ] Data processing functions created
- [ ] Basic styling applied

**Next week (Week 8):**
- Final features
- Testing
- Deployment to shinylive
- Polish and documentation
:::

## Tips for Success

::: {.incremental}
* **Start with working code**, then add features
* **Test frequently** - don't wait until the end
* **Keep it simple** - focus on functionality
* **Document as you go** - explain your choices
* **Ask for help early** - don't struggle alone
:::

## Summary

::: {.incremental}
* **OSMnx**: Download and analyze street networks
* **Network metrics**: Betweenness, closeness, degree
* **r5py**: Fast multi-modal routing
* **Shiny integration**: Interactive network dashboards
* **Assignment 2**: Use these tools effectively
:::

## Next Week

**Week 8: Advanced Dashboards + Deployment**

::: {.incremental}
* Temporal analysis in dashboards
* Shinylive deployment walkthrough
* Assignment 2 finalization
* Testing and debugging
:::

## Resources

* OSMnx documentation: geoffboeing.com/publications/osmnx-paper
* r5py documentation: r5py.readthedocs.io
* NetworkX tutorial: networkx.org/documentation/stable/tutorial.html
* Shiny for Python: shiny.posit.co/py

## Thanks! <br> Q & A {style="text-align: center;"}

**Work time now!**

Get started on:
- Network analysis for Assignment 2
- Dashboard improvements
- Data processing

<div style="width:840px"><iframe allow="fullscreen" frameBorder="0" height="480" src="https://giphy.com/embed/3oKIPnAiaMCws8nOsE/video" width="840"></iframe></div>
